query: Reineke self-thinning rule arboriculture stand density diameter relationship 2024

papers.title: Dynamics of stand density and self-thinning in Chinese fir plantations: theoretical insights and empirical validation
papers.notes: PMC-indexed, real URL. Empirical 2024 validation of Reineke's frontier slope.

papers.title: Investigating the Self-Thinning Rule in Plantation Forests: Analyzing the Relationship Between the Basal Area and Height Growth in Southern China
papers.notes: Recent 2024 study; expands rule from diameter to basal-area / height-growth.

papers.title: Comparison and analysis of self-thinning models based on diameter-based maximum size-density relationships
papers.notes: 2025 review comparing multiple self-thinning model formulations.

papers.title: Comparison and analysis of self-thinning models (ScienceDirect 2024)
papers.notes: 2024 paper formalizing self-thinning frontier and slope variance across species.

mechanism_summary: Reineke's rule: max stand density N_max and mean diameter D obey log(N_max) = -1.605 * log(D) + k along a frontier in log-log space. Stands evolve along this frontier as competition kills the smallest stems; the frontier is the 'null space' of viable (N, D) configurations. Slope varies -2.33 to -1.54 across species in 2024 empirical work.
